meta.name: Roasted Vegetables with Olive Oil, Garlic, and Herbs

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Mediterranean baseline side: high-veg, olive oil + herbs + optional lemon finish.
Designed as a reusable template for planners (swap seasonal vegetables).

yield.notes: Makes ~1 full sheet pan. Scale for multiple pans rather than overcrowding.

ingredients.label: Eggplant, cut into 1-inch chunks
ingredients.notes: If eggplant is very spongy, salt 10 minutes and pat dry to reduce oil absorption.

ingredients.label: Cherry tomatoes (optional), left whole
ingredients.notes: Add halfway through roasting to prevent bursting into sauce.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use enough to lightly coat; avoid pooling oil on pan.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: About 3–4 cloves. For less risk of scorching, add in the last 10 minutes.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste and vegetable water content.

ingredients.label: Lemon (zest and/or juice), to finish
ingredients.notes: A bright finish is very Mediterranean; add to taste.

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a sheet pan with parchment (optional) for easier cleanup. For best browning, use a large pan and avoid overcrowding.

steps.title: Cut vegetables evenly
steps.body: Cut zucchini, eggplant, peppers, and red onion into roughly 1-inch pieces so they roast at the same rate. Pat eggplant dry if salted or very wet.

steps.title: Toss with olive oil, herbs, salt, and pepper
steps.body: In a large bowl, toss vegetables with olive oil, oregano, thyme/rosemary (optional), salt, and pepper (optional) until lightly coated. Add garlic now if you prefer a stronger garlic roast flavor, or reserve it for the last 10 minutes to prevent scorching.

steps.title: Roast until starting to brown
steps.body: Spread vegetables in a single layer on the sheet pan (do not pile). Roast 15 minutes, then flip/stir with a spatula to expose new surfaces for browning.

steps.title: Add cherry tomatoes (optional) and finish roasting
steps.body: If using cherry tomatoes, add them now and stir gently. If reserving garlic, add it now and toss lightly. Return to oven and roast until vegetables are tender with browned edges.

steps.title: Finish with lemon and herbs (optional) and serve
steps.body: Transfer to a serving bowl. If desired, finish with lemon zest and/or juice and chopped parsley. Taste and adjust salt. Serve warm or at room temperature.

refrigerate.notes: Reheat in a hot oven or skillet to re-crisp edges. Microwave works but softens texture.

freeze.notes: Freezing is possible but texture softens; best for blending into soups/sauces.

variations.title: Seasonal vegetable swaps
changes: Winter: use cauliflower, carrots, Brussels sprouts, sweet potatoes (adjust roast time +10–15 mins).
Spring: asparagus + fennel + baby potatoes (add asparagus late).
Fall: butternut squash + red onion + mushrooms (add mushrooms late).

variations.title: Paprika + cumin Mediterranean profile (warm spice)
changes: Add 2 g paprika and 1 g ground cumin with the oregano.
Finish with lemon juice to keep it bright.

variations.title: Feta finish (not vegan)
changes: Crumble 60–100 g feta over hot vegetables right before serving.
Reduce salt slightly since feta adds salinity.

variations.title: Balsamic finish (sweet/tangy)
changes: Drizzle 10–15 ml balsamic vinegar (or reduced balsamic glaze) after roasting.
Pairs well with peppers and onions.

notes: Do not overcrowd: steam is the enemy of browning. Use two pans if doubling.
425°F / 220°C gives good caramelization. If your oven runs hot, use 410°F / 210°C.
If garlic scorches easily in your oven, add garlic during the final 8–10 minutes.
